items.title: Apple Stores Will Soon Be Able to Restore Apple Watch Software In-House
items.summary: Apple retail locations and Apple Authorized Service Providers will soon be able to restore Apple Watch software in-store without needing to send an Apple Watch to a service center, according to a retail source that spoke to MacRumors ....

items.title: iPhone users no longer able to downgrade to iOS 26.4
items.summary: Following last week's release of iOS 26.4.1, Apple today stopped signing iOS 26.4. Here's what that means.

items.title: Deepfake nonconsensual porn apps are advertising in the App Store
items.summary: Users looking to create nonconsensual deepfakes of unknowing individuals can simply perform an App Store search. If App Review has a job, it clearly isn't doing it. It is trivially easy to search for deepfake tools when they take out ads...

items.title: iOS 26.4 No Longer Signed by Apple, Blocking Downgrades From iOS 26.4.1
items.summary: Apple today stopped signing iOS 26.4, so iPhone users who have updated to iOS 26.4.1 are no longer able to downgrade to the earlier version of iOS. iOS 26.4.1 came out a week ago . When software is "signed," it means it can pass the...

items.title: Ford dissolves EV unit, Doug Field out as Farley chases 8% margin
items.summary: Ford is dissolving its standalone electric vehicle division - and losing the executive who built it. The automaker said today that Doug Field, its chief EV, digital, and design officer, will leave the company next month after nearly five...

items.title: Ford EV and tech chief leaving automaker
items.summary: Doug Field had stints at Apple and Tesla before joining Ford in 2021.

items.title: Memory cards and flash drives prices rocket 124%, some products peak at 261% jump - increases from 2025 driven by AI chip shortage across a range of formats and capacities
items.summary: USB flash drives, SD cards, and microSD cards are becoming expensive as the NAND chip shortage takes its bite and expands to other storage options.
